description: Migrate git hooks from husky + lint-staged to lefthook. Replace the husky/lint-staged npm packages and .husky/ directory with a single lefthook.yml config. Preserve all existing hook behavior: pre-commit (ralph state file guard, lockfile auto-staging, biome formatting of staged files) and pre-push (build, build:plugins, type-check, test). Update package.json, CLAUDE.md, and CONTRIBUTING.md to reflect the new tooling.

userStories.title: Install lefthook and create lefthook.yml with all hooks
userStories.description: Remove husky and lint-staged from devDependencies, remove the lint-staged config and prepare script from package.json, delete the .husky/ directory, install lefthook, and create lefthook.yml with equivalent pre-commit and pre-push hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: lefthook is in devDependencies in root package.json
husky and lint-staged are NOT in devDependencies
No lint-staged config block in package.json
prepare script runs 'lefthook install' instead of 'husky'
lefthook.yml exists at repo root with pre-commit and pre-push hooks
The .husky/ directory is deleted (both user hooks and _/ infrastructure)
Pre-commit hook auto-stages dirty package-lock.json whose sibling package.json is staged
Pre-commit hook runs biome check --write on staged *.ts and *.tsx files
Pre-commit hook runs biome format --write on staged *.json and *.md files
Pre-push hook runs npm run build, npm run build:plugins, npm run type-check, npm run test sequentially
userStories.notes:
Files:
- package.json (remove husky + lint-staged deps, remove lint-staged config, change prepare script)
- lefthook.yml (new file — the entire hook configuration)
- .husky/pre-commit (delete)
- .husky/pre-push (delete)
- .husky/_/ (delete entire directory)

Context:
The repo currently uses husky v9 + lint-staged v16 for git hooks. Husky installs shims in .husky/_/ that delegate to user scripts in .husky/. lint-staged stashes unstaged changes, runs biome on staged files, and pops the stash. lefthook replaces both — it has built-in staged-file filtering (`glob` + `run` per command) and does not need a separate tool like lint-staged.

Changes:

1. Remove from package.json devDependencies: `"husky": "^9.1.7"` and `"lint-staged": "^16.2.7"`
2. Remove the entire `"lint-staged"` config block (lines 74-84 in package.json)
3. Change `"prepare": "husky"` to `"prepare": "lefthook install"`
4. Add `"lefthook": "^1.11.13"` to devDependencies (or latest stable — check npm)
5. Delete the entire `.husky/` directory (including `.husky/_/` with all shims)
6. Create `lefthook.yml` at repo root

The lefthook.yml must replicate all current behavior. Here is the target structure:

```yaml
pre-commit:
  commands:
    reject-ralph-state:
      run: |
        if git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '\.ralph/(prd\.json|progress\.txt)$'; then
          echo "ERROR: Ralph state files must not be committed:"
          git diff --cached --name-only | grep -E '\.ralph/(prd\.json|progress\.txt)$'
          echo ""
          echo "Run: git rm --cached <file> to untrack them."
          exit 1
        fi
    auto-stage-lockfiles:
      run: |
        for pkg in $(git diff --cached --name-only | grep 'package\.json$'); do
          lockfile="$(dirname "$pkg")/package-lock.json"
          if [ -f "$lockfile" ] && git diff --name-only -- "$lockfile" | grep -q .; then
            git add "$lockfile"
          fi
        done
    biome-check-ts:
      glob: "*.{ts,tsx}"
      run: biome check --write --no-errors-on-unmatched {staged_files}
      stage_fixed: true
    biome-format-json:
      glob: "*.json"
      run: biome format --write --no-errors-on-unmatched {staged_files}
      stage_fixed: true
    biome-format-md:
      glob: "*.md"
      run: biome format --write --no-errors-on-unmatched {staged_files}
      stage_fixed: true

pre-push:
  commands:
    build:
      run: npm run build
    build-plugins:
      run: npm run build:plugins
    type-check:
      run: npm run type-check
    test:
      run: npm run test
  parallel: false
```

Key lefthook features used:
- `glob` + `{staged_files}` replaces lint-staged's staged file filtering
- `stage_fixed: true` re-stages files after biome auto-fixes them (equivalent to lint-staged's behavior)
- `parallel: false` on pre-push ensures sequential execution (build before type-check before test)
- Shell script commands for reject-ralph-state and auto-stage-lockfiles preserve existing bash logic

After removing packages, run `npm install` to update the lockfile, then run `npm install lefthook` (or add manually and run `npm install`).

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any biome configuration
- Do NOT change any npm scripts other than 'prepare'
- The ralph state file rejection and lockfile auto-staging must behave identically to the current .husky/pre-commit
- Pre-push commands must run in the exact same order: build, build:plugins, type-check, test
- lefthook.yml must be committed to the repo (it is the config, not generated infrastructure)

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md and CONTRIBUTING.md to reflect lefthook
userStories.description: Update all documentation references from husky/lint-staged to lefthook. Remove the lint-staged Stash Hazard section from CLAUDE.md (lefthook does not have this problem). Update CONTRIBUTING.md's Git Hooks section.
acceptanceCriteria: No references to 'husky' or 'lint-staged' in CLAUDE.md
No references to 'husky' or 'lint-staged' in CONTRIBUTING.md
The 'lint-staged Stash Hazard' section is removed from CLAUDE.md
CONTRIBUTING.md Git Hooks section describes lefthook
The Lockfile Auto-Staging section in CLAUDE.md is preserved (behavior unchanged, just tool name updated if referenced)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- CLAUDE.md (update lint-staged Stash Hazard section, update any husky/lint-staged references)
- CONTRIBUTING.md (update Git Hooks section)

Context:
CLAUDE.md has a section '### lint-staged Stash Hazard' at line 326-337 that documents a specific problem with lint-staged's stash/pop behavior. lefthook does NOT have this problem — it uses a different mechanism for staged file isolation. This entire section should be removed.

The 'Lockfile Auto-Staging' section at line 322-324 documents the pre-commit hook behavior of auto-staging lockfiles. This behavior is preserved in lefthook.yml, so the section content stays but should not reference husky or lint-staged.

Changes to CLAUDE.md:
1. Remove the entire '### lint-staged Stash Hazard' section (lines 326-337), including the 'After every commit where you have unstaged changes, verify they survived:' paragraph and the code block.
2. Scan for any remaining references to 'husky' or 'lint-staged' and update them. The current CLAUDE.md does not mention 'husky' by name in the sections I've read, but the lint-staged reference at line 326 and 328 must be removed.

Changes to CONTRIBUTING.md:
1. Line 123: Change `The project uses [Husky](https://typicode.github.io/husky/) for git hooks:` to describe lefthook instead: `The project uses [Lefthook](https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook) for git hooks:`
2. Line 128: Change `Runs \`lint-staged\`: Biome check (lint + format) on staged \`.ts\`, \`.tsx\`, and \`.json\` files; Biome format on \`.md\` files` to describe biome running directly via lefthook (no lint-staged wrapper)
3. Keep the rest of the Git Hooks section structure (Pre-commit, Pre-push) but update tool names

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any sections unrelated to git hooks
- Preserve the Lockfile Auto-Staging section content (behavior is unchanged)
- Preserve the Concurrent AI Work section (it follows the lint-staged section and is unrelated)
- Do NOT add lefthook installation instructions — the prepare script handles it
